channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance gets his long-awaited moment to admonish Ukraine's Zelenskyy
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vice President JD Vance was dismissing Ukraine long before he upended an Oval Office meeting Friday by calling Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy “disrespectful” and asking if he had ever thanked the U.S. for its support. When Vance was a candidate for U.S.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: See the star-studded list of Trump allies descending on DC to chart further 100-day wins
item.description: A Rick Scott-tied group is hosting an event with an all-star line-up of MAGA world figures to discuss how they can further assist President Trump to score more wins before his 100th day in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Newly elected lawmakers reflect on their first legislative session
item.description: MPR News host Brian Bakst talks with two state representatives serving in their first legislative session. Later, a conversation with State Auditor Julie Blaha.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Most Americans who experienced severe winter weather see climate change at work, AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Matt Ries has lived in Florida only three years, but everyone told him last summer was unusually hot. That was followed by three hurricanes in close succession. Then temperatures dropped below freezing for days this winter, and snow blanketed part of the state.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and AMELIA THOMSON-DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia gov promises 'full cooperation' with ICE to deport illegal immigrants
item.description: Virginia became the latest of a handful of mostly GOP states that are compelling law enforcement officers to work with federal authorities to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's early backing of Ramaswamy for Ohio governor seen by some as meant to avoid a nasty primary
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The sun hadn't set on Republican Vivek Ramaswamy's gubernatorial campaign launch in Ohio earlier this week before President Donald Trump posted his endorsement of the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur and former Department of Government Efficiency co-chair.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AG Pam Bondi demands 'full' Epstein files from FBI, vows investigation
item.description: The DOJ released hundreds of Epstein-related files Thursday to a small group of conservative influencers, but Bondi says there's still more to be uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: President Donald Trump berated Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved that country closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge finds mass firings of federal probationary workers were likely unlawful
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco on Thursday found that the mass firings of probationary employees were likely unlawful, granting temporary relief to a coalition of labor unions and organizations that has sued to stop the Trump administration’s massive dismantling of the federal
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of weather forecasters fired in latest wave of DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of weather forecasters and other federal National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration employees on probationary status were fired Thursday, lawmakers and weather experts said.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress votes to kill Biden-era methane fee on oil and gas producers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Republican-controlled Congress has voted to repeal a federal fee on oil and gas producers who release high levels of methane, undoing a major piece of former President Joe Biden's climate policy aimed at controlling the planet-warming “super pollutant.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 700 National Park Service employees are taking buyouts: reports
item.description: Earlier this month, the Trump administration fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: I 'wouldn't be opposed' to using private forces for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he "wouldn't be opposed" to using private forces to assist with deportations.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Giving US ownership of Ukraine's minerals would help keep peace: Hassett
item.description: Kevin Hassett said in an interview with NewsNation's Kellie Meyer that these minerals' estimated worth is $20B.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada: Here's when
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting next Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, UK's Keir Starmer speak after meetings
item.description: Starmer is expected to try to convince Trump that Ukraine and European leaders need a place at the table for peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says federal workers should expect another round of ‘pulse check’ emails
item.description: President Trump warned federal workers who did not reply to recent emails asking them to describe “five things” they accomplished are “on the bubble” suggesting they are at risk of losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Heidi Glenn, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens immigrants who don't register with fines, prison
item.description: DHS states everyone who's in the U.S. without documentation must register, give fingerprints and provide an address.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrat proposes study on slavery's impact, possible reparations
item.description: A Nevada Democratic assemblymember is proposing a bill to create a commission to study discrimination against Black Americans and possible reparations.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
